metadata.scope: all repo Python files excluding .git, .venv, dist, build, and caches
metadata.purpose: Tree-shaped inventory of package, test, docs-tooling, script, and asset-tooling Python objects needing docstring improvement.
criteria: Package src: missing docstrings on public/provisional classes, functions, methods, properties, and selected lifecycle/magic methods.
Package src: thin generic one-line docstrings and thin design-sensitive docstrings on tensorization, tower, runtime, adapter, fiber, and public example surfaces.
Tests: missing or too-thin module/test/helper docstrings where scenario intent, behavioral contract, or regression purpose is not explicit.
Docs tooling, repo scripts, and asset tooling: missing or too-thin module/function/class docstrings where command purpose, inputs, outputs, and side effects are not explicit.

scope_tiers.tests: pytest modules and test helpers under tests/.
scope_tiers.docs_tooling: Python tooling under docs/ if present.
scope_tiers.repo_scripts: Python command scripts under scripts/ if present.
scope_tiers.other_python: Other tracked Python files not matched by the above tiers.

notes: Private helpers are excluded unless they are selected lifecycle/magic methods, test functions, or public helper objects.
D202 blank-line formatting findings are intentionally excluded; this inventory focuses on semantic docstring improvement work.
Thin-docstring findings are heuristic review candidates, not automatic defects.
Test docstrings should be useful scenario documentation, not boilerplate restatements of test names.
